title: Add intent generator tool and capability for fail-close review

in: Python tool to scan repository and generate intent JSONs for commits missing governance intents
New intentGenerator capability integrated into Auernyx system
Allowlist and policy updates to register new capability
out: No changes to receipt format or contract schemas
No modifications to existing capability logic
No changes to ledger or kintsugi integrity checks

verification.plan: 1. Run npm run compile to verify TypeScript compilation. 2. Run npm run verify to test core capabilities. 3. Test intent_generator.py with --scan and --commit flags. 4. Verify CI gate passes with exactly one intent file. 5. Confirm router maps 'generate intent' to intentGenerator capability.

evidence.notes:
Intent for commit ccc10df2cc7c1bea4d93ab526afe913d8c8aa84a

This change adds tooling to help review commits that failed CI due to missing intent files. The tool can scan git history, classify changes, and generate properly formatted intent JSON files.

Files modified:
- capabilities/intentGenerator.ts (new capability)
- config/allowlist.json (added intentGenerator)
- core/policy.ts (added intentGenerator to CapabilityName type and metadata)
- core/router.ts (added intent routing for 'generate intent' commands)
- core/server.ts (registered intentGenerator in capability map)
- tools/README.md (added documentation)
- tools/intent_generator.py (main Python script with scan and generation logic)

Additional files:
- docs/FAIL_CLOSE_REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md (workflow documentation)
- tools/process_failed_runs.py (GitHub Actions integration helper)

Risk mitigation: The capability is tier 1 (non-critical), requires approval, and only generates files—it does not modify existing governance data structures.
